signal/ITIMER_PROF.name: ITIMER_PROF
signal/ITIMER_PROF.description: Decrements interval timer both when the process executes and when the system is executing on behalf of the process. Coupled with ITIMER_VIRTUAL, this timer is usually used to profile the time spent by the application in user and kernel space. SIGPROF is delivered upon expiration.

info.excerpt: Decrements interval timer both when the process executes and when the system is executing on behalf of the process. Coupled with ITIMER_VIRTUAL, this timer is usually used to profile the time spent by...

signal/ITIMER_REAL.name: ITIMER_REAL
signal/ITIMER_REAL.description: Decrements interval timer in real time, and delivers SIGALRM upon expiration.

info.excerpt: Decrements interval timer in real time, and delivers SIGALRM upon expiration.

signal/ITIMER_VIRTUAL.name: ITIMER_VIRTUAL
signal/ITIMER_VIRTUAL.description: Decrements interval timer only when the process is executing, and delivers SIGVTALRM upon expiration.

info.excerpt: Decrements interval timer only when the process is executing, and delivers SIGVTALRM upon expiration.

signal/ItimerError.name: ItimerError
signal/ItimerError.description: Raised to signal an error from the underlying setitimer() or getitimer() implementation. Expect this error if an invalid interval timer or a negative time is passed to setitimer(). This error is a subtype of OSError. Added in version 3.3: This error used to be a subtype of IOError, which is now an alias of OSError.
